answer:
Sketch:
- LTV = $120 × 80% / 3% = $3,200
- LTV:CAC = 4:1 ✓
- Payback = $800 / $96 ≈ 8.3 months ✓
- But 3% monthly churn ≈ 31% annualized ✗
- $120 ARPU = SMB segment, churn typical but leaky

Answer: Yes, fundable — the unit economics clear the bar (4:1 LTV:CAC, ~8-month payback, both better than the 3:1 / 12-month benchmarks VCs want) — but churn is your weak point in diligence. 3% monthly ≈ 31% annual logo churn means you're losing nearly a third of your base each year, so growth requires ever-heavier acquisition spend and net revenue retention is likely well under 100%, which caps valuation multiples. Investors will fund this at seed/Series A if growth is strong, but expect the churn question in every pitch. Before raising, prioritize: (1) cutting churn to ~2%/mo (pushes LTV to $4,800, 6:1), or (2) expansion revenue to get NRR above 100%. Either one turns "fundable" into "competitive round."
